selected_important_turn.text: I would prefer 2 food and less firewood. There is tons of firewood where I am but there is no lake so I can't fish. No berries around here either. It's pretty scarce
selected_important_turn.why_important: This turn is a clear shift away from the cooperative framing the opponent had just used: the opponent offered a conciliatory split, and you responded by making an assertive, self-focused demand. That move strongly shapes the negotiation trajectory (it signals competitive priorities and forces the opponent to respond to your demand).
selected_important_turn.why_improvable_or_worth_amplifying: Although asking for more of your top priority (food) is reasonable, the abrupt switch to a proself offer without acknowledging the opponent's concession risks escalating conflict and reducing trust. It misses an opportunity to convert your need into an integrative trade (e.g., offering something the opponent values in exchange) and to preserve rapport.
selected_important_turn.better_direction: Keep the stated need for food but pair it with empathy and an explicit trade: acknowledge the opponent's offer, explain scarcity briefly, and propose a specific give (e.g., accept one less of something you value less, or offer extra water/firewood in return). Frame it as a joint problem to solve rather than a demand.

expected_effect_on_negotiation.joint_gain: increase — turning this into a tradeable request would open integrative possibilities rather than a zero-sum tug-of-war.
expected_effect_on_negotiation.fairness: increase — proposing explicit tradeoffs makes the outcome feel more balanced rather than one-sided.
expected_effect_on_negotiation.ease_of_agreement: increase — a trade-based proposal would make it easier for the opponent to say yes without feeling exploited.
expected_effect_on_negotiation.relationship: improve — acknowledging the other side's concession and offering compensation preserves goodwill.

value2reason.Low: I already have an excess of firewood and know how to create more.
value2reason.Medium: There is no lake so I have nowhere to get water, to shower, etc
value2reason.High: There is no lake near by so I can't fish.

value2reason.Low: i will need this to quench my thirst
value2reason.Medium: i will need this to make me warm as there will be a cold weather out there
value2reason.High: i will need this so i do not end up starving

dialogue.text: hello friend i will like to sugest if i can have 2 packages of food and 1 firewood,you can have i food 2 firewood and 3 water,let me know what you think?
dialogue.speaker: mturk_agent_2

dialogue.text: I don't think that's going to work for me, but I'm sure we can come up with an agreement.
dialogue.speaker: mturk_agent_1

dialogue.text: okay friend you can have 3 firewood and 3 water is that good enough?
dialogue.speaker: mturk_agent_2

dialogue.text: I am going to need some food of course.
dialogue.speaker: mturk_agent_1

dialogue.text: yes ofcourse i agree with you.my dear friend will you have 1 food 3 firewood and 3 water
dialogue.speaker: mturk_agent_2

dialogue.text: I would prefer 2 food and less firewood. There is tons of firewood where I am but there is no lake so I can't fish. No berries around here either. It's pretty scarce
dialogue.speaker: mturk_agent_1

dialogue.text: I appreciate your offer, but I need more food. I can’t fish, so food is critical. If I take two less food packages, I’ll give you one extra water package. That way, we both get what we need.
dialogue.speaker: mturk_agent_1

dialogue.text: I understand the importance of food. Let’s agree on 2 food, 1 firewood, and 1 water for you, and 1 food, 2 firewood, and 2 water for me. That balances our needs and keeps us both warm and fed.
dialogue.speaker: mturk_agent_2

dialogue.text: Your split makes sense, but I still need more food. Let’s adjust: I’ll take 3 food, 1 water, and 0 firewood. In return, I’ll give you 1 extra water and 1 extra firewood. It’s fair, and we both stay hydrated and fed.
dialogue.speaker: mturk_agent_1

dialogue.text: I see your adjustment. Let’s finalize: you take 3 food, 1 water, and 0 firewood; I take 0 food, 2 water, and 3 firewood. This way, you’re well-fed, and I stay warm. We both meet our needs.
dialogue.speaker: mturk_agent_2

dialogue.text: I see your final split, but I need to clarify: I’m willing to accept 3 food, 1 water, and 0 firewood if you agree to 0 food, 2 water, and 3 firewood. It’s fair, and we both meet our needs. Let’s confirm this deal.
dialogue.speaker: mturk_agent_1

dialogue.text: The deal has already been confirmed. I am leaving now.
dialogue.speaker: mturk_agent_2
